name: Old Goa Churches
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with baroque churches

name: Anjuna Beach
description: Iconic rocky beach known for its flea market and trance parties

name: Calangute Beach
description: The 'Queen of Beaches', highly commercialized and lively

name: Palolem Beach
description: Scenic crescent-shaped beach lined with leaning palm trees

name: Arambol Beach
description: Bohemian vibe beach with drum circles and alternative culture

name: Chapora Fort
description: Historic hill fort offering majestic views of the Arabian Sea (Dil Chahta Hai fort)

name: Spice Plantation
description: Guided tours through lush aromatic spice farms with authentic Goan lunch

name: Colva Beach
description: Expansive white sand beach popular among domestic tourists in South Goa

name: Vagator Beach
description: Red cliff beach split into 'Big Vagator' and 'Little Vagator'

name: Panaji Market
description: Bustling local market selling cashews, spices, port wine, and Goan sweets

name: Grand Island
description: Premiere destination for scuba diving and snorkeling in Goa

name: Butterfly Beach
description: Secluded cove accessible mainly by boat, famous for butterfly spotting

name: Divar Island
description: Sleepy river island giving a glimpse of old-world traditional Goa
